claim_text: LiteLLM + Istio + OpenMeter should be recommended path
counterargument: Three-plane stack can overfit large orgs and overburden small teams
boundary_condition_not_hold: No dedicated platform/SRE team and low request scale
recommendation_for_c: Mark as scale-conditional architecture not universal default

claim_text: Do not use single gateway for all controls
counterargument: In early phase, single gateway with strict guardrails may be sufficient and faster
boundary_condition_not_hold: Single provider, low compliance burden, low QPS
recommendation_for_c: Allow temporary single-gateway baseline with upgrade trigger

counterargument: Some internal tools can accept lower SLA if correctness and cost are priority
recommendation_for_c: Define tiered SLA by product criticality

counterargument: 1% may be unrealistic before ledger normalization and token accounting harmonization
boundary_condition_not_hold: Early rollout with mixed provider billing semantics

claim_text: Retry forbidden for non-idempotent ops is enough safety
counterargument: Idempotency classification can be wrong; retries can still leak side effects indirectly
boundary_condition_not_hold: Tool wrappers without strict idempotency metadata
recommendation_for_c: Require explicit idempotency tags and policy tests

counterargument: Fixed canary ladder may be too slow for urgent patches or too risky for fragile systems
boundary_condition_not_hold: Hotfix window or high blast-radius endpoints

counterargument: Dual-layer can create conflicting enforcement and operator confusion without ownership split
boundary_condition_not_hold: No clear source-of-truth between gateway and ledger
recommendation_for_c: Define authoritative system per decision type

claim_text: Upper gateway route + lower mesh resilience is required
counterargument: Mesh layer may be unnecessary in simple monolith or single-cluster setups
boundary_condition_not_hold: No east-west traffic complexity or service mesh capability
recommendation_for_c: Allow envoy-only or gateway-only minimal profile

claim_text: Without trace_id + cost attribution + SLO alerting system is not production controllable
counterargument: Certain batch/offline pipelines can be production-acceptable with job-level telemetry
recommendation_for_c: Split observability requirements by online vs batch

claim_text: Protocol moved from popularity to conformance+tier
counterargument: Community adoption still strongly influences practical compatibility and issue response speed
boundary_condition_not_hold: Conformance claims without active host ecosystem validation
recommendation_for_c: Use blended score: conformance + adoption + incident history

counterargument: Some hosts/servers may lag in notifications support while still usable
boundary_condition_not_hold: Legacy clients lacking full capability notifications
recommendation_for_c: Treat notifications as preferred not absolute blocker in transition period

claim_text: Registry + runtime dual-check should gate production enablement
counterargument: Over-strict gating may stall urgent integrations and reduce experiment velocity
boundary_condition_not_hold: Time-critical onboarding with trusted internal servers
recommendation_for_c: Provide emergency fast-track with post-audit SLA

claim_text: Transport baseline should be streamable HTTP
counterargument: Local stdio can be more reliable and simpler for desktop/locality-sensitive tools
boundary_condition_not_hold: Developer workstation or air-gapped local scenarios
recommendation_for_c: Choose transport by trust boundary and deployment model

claim_text: Tool naming collision policy should be strict namespace format
counterargument: Overly rigid naming may hurt third-party compatibility and migration
boundary_condition_not_hold: Integrations with legacy tool naming contracts
recommendation_for_c: Add alias mapping layer instead of hard reject

counterargument: Hard-fail everywhere can break resilience for non-critical optional tools
boundary_condition_not_hold: Best-effort auxiliary tools where graceful degradation preferred
recommendation_for_c: Use fail mode by tool criticality class

claim_text: No strategy gate for external MCP server should be avoided
counterargument: For sandboxed read-only servers, lighter gate can be acceptable
boundary_condition_not_hold: Read-only low-risk servers with no credentials and strict sandbox

claim_text: OpenMeter is weak-related but should be kept
counterargument: In low-scale contexts, spreadsheet-level monthly budget governance may be sufficient initially
boundary_condition_not_hold: Small team with fixed budget and low model diversity

claim_text: License gate unresolved means cannot go production
counterargument: Emergency cases may require temporary exception with compensating controls
boundary_condition_not_hold: Critical outage requiring immediate mitigation dependency
recommendation_for_c: Allow time-bound exception with signed risk acceptance
